content.Text:
[Mon 2026-04-20 01:07 PDT] Create a new file `docs/expiry-watchdog.html` — a self-contained single-file interactive HTML dashboard called "Reagent Expiry Watchdog" for the BioBots bioprinting lab platform.

## Design Requirements
- Match the existing dark theme: `--bg:#0f172a; --card:#1e293b; --border:#334155; --text:#e2e8f0; --dim:#94a3b8; --green:#22c55e; --yellow:#eab308; --orange:#f97316; --red:#ef4444`
- Single HTML file, no external dependencies. All CSS inline in `<style>`, all JS inline in `<script>`.
- Responsive grid layout.

## Feature: Reagent Expiry Watchdog

An autonomous monitoring dashboard that tracks reagent/consumable expiration dates and proactively manages lab inventory lifecycle.

### Core Functionality:

1. **Reagent Registry** — Add reagents with: name, category (bioink/crosslinker/reagent/media/enzyme/buffer/other), lot number, quantity, unit, received date, expiry date, storage condition (RT/4°C/-20°C/-80°C/LN2), location (shelf/fridge/freezer ID)

2. **Expiry Timeline** — Canvas-based horizontal timeline showing all reagents as colored bars. Color by urgency: green (>30 days), yellow (15-30 days), orange (7-15 days), red (<7 days), dark red/strikethrough (expired). Click a bar to see details.

3. **Watchdog Dashboard Cards** (top area):
   - Total items tracked
   - Expired count (red)
   - Expiring within 7 days (orange) 
   - Expiring within 30 days (yellow)
   - Waste risk score (0-100, based on qty×urgency of expiring items)
   - Estimated waste cost (assume $10-50/item based on category)

4. **Proactive Alerts Panel** — Auto-generated recommendations:
   - "Use [reagent] within [N] days or risk $X waste"
   - "Consider reordering [reagent] — current stock expires [date]"
   - "Dispose expired [reagent] (Lot: XXX) — expired [N] days ago"
   - "Storage audit needed: [reagent] at [location] approaching expiry cluster"
   - Priority-sorted by urgency

5. **Auto-Triage Mode** — Toggle button. When enabled, runs every 5 seconds simulated:
   - Scans all items
   - Updates urgency classifications
   - Generates new alerts if status changed
   - Shows a pulsing green dot when active

6. **Disposal Queue** — Items past expiry auto-added to disposal queue with checkboxes for batch disposal confirmation. Track disposal history.

7. **Usage Forecasting** — For each reagent, user can log usage events. Based on average consumption rate, predict when stock will run out vs when it expires. Show "Will expire before use" or "Will run out before expiry" status.

8. **Seed Data** — Pre-populate with 15 realistic bioprinting reagents (alginate, gelatin, collagen, fibrinogen, CaCl2, thrombin, DMEM, FBS, trypsin, PBS, pluronic F-127, hyaluronic acid, matrigel, polyethylene glycol, genipin) with realistic expiry dates ranging from already expired to 90 days out.

9. **Export** — JSON export of full inventory state. CSV export of alerts.

### UI Layout:
- Title bar: "🔬 Reagent Expiry Watchdog" with subtitle "Autonomous expiry monitoring • Waste prevention • Reorder intelligence"  
- Auto-triage toggle in top-right
- Dashboard cards row
- Alerts panel (left 60%) | Disposal queue (right 40%)
- Canvas timeline (full width)
- Reagent table with sort/filter
- Add reagent form (collapsible)
- Log usage form (collapsible)

### Implementation Notes:
- Use localStorage for persistence
- All dates relative to `new Date()` for seed data so it always looks current
- Canvas timeline should be interactive (hover tooltips, click to select)
- The watchdog/auto-triage concept is the key agentic element — it acts autonomously

Also update `docs/sitemap.xml` to add an entry for `expiry-watchdog.html`.

Also update `docs/index.html` — find the tools grid and add a new card linking to `expiry-watchdog.html` with title "Reagent Expiry Watchdog" and description "Autonomous expiry monitoring with waste prevention and reorder intelligence". Match the existing card style. Add it in alphabetical order among the existing cards.
